trade.summary: The EAU is India's third-largest trading partner and second-largest export destination. India exports gems, jewelry, textiles, machinery, and food to the EAU. The EAU exports crude oil, refined petroleum, gold, and silver to India. A Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) entered into force in May 2022, targeting $100B trade by 2030.

diplomatic.summary: Relations are at an all-time high. The EAU hosts approximately 3.5 million Indians — the largest expatriate community in the EAU. Indian PM Modi has visited EAU multiple times. The two countries signed the CEPA in 2022, and cooperate on counter-terrorism, regional security, and MENASA connectivity. The EAU backed India's position on Kashmir in key moments.

military.summary: India and the EAU conduct joint military exercises (Desert Eagle). The Indian Navy has access to EAU ports. Defense cooperation is growing with India offering defense technology exports and the EAU interested in Indian-made military equipment. India helped secure release of Indian sailors detained in EAU territorial incidents.

financial.summary: EAU is one of India's largest sources of remittances (~$15B annually from the large Indian diaspora). EAU sovereign funds (ADIA, Mubadala, ICD) are among the largest foreign investors in India. Mubadala invested $1.2B in Reliance Industries. ADIA participates in Indian infrastructure. The Indian rupee is accepted in EAU gold markets.

energy.summary: The EAU is one of India's top crude oil suppliers. India imports approximately 7-8% of its crude from the EAU. ADNOC has a strategic petroleum reserve in India (Padur). Indian Oil and other refiners have long-term supply agreements with ADNOC.

scientific.summary: Strong collaboration in IT, pharmaceuticals, and space. Indian tech companies dominate EAU's IT sector. India's space program (ISRO) has collaborated with EAU's space agency. Hundreds of Indian universities have partnerships with EAU institutions.
